commit.message:
Update CHANGELOG for v1.2.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add latency monitor, extract NetSpeedKit, add 75 tests

Latency feature:
- LatencyMonitor: measures TCP handshake RTT via NWConnection (no root needed)
- Targets Cloudflare/Google/Apple DNS, 5-probe rolling average
- Latency displayed in popover with color-coded status pill
- Optional menu bar latency display
- Settings: enable/disable, ping target, show in menu bar
- Integrated with sleep/wake cycle

Architecture refactor:
- Extract NetSpeedKit library with public testable types:
  NetworkTrafficReader, TrafficSnapshot, SpeedFormatter, LatencyMonitor
- ViewModel delegates to SpeedFormatter for all formatting
- Package.swift: executable depends on NetSpeedKit library

Test suite (75 tests, all passing):
- TrafficSnapshot delta: basic, multi-interface, rollover, edge cases
- Interface filtering: en*/pdp_ip* allowed, utun/bridge/llw/lo rejected
- NetworkTrafficReader live: snapshot non-nil, timestamp, interface filter
- SpeedFormatter: bytes, bits, compact, latency, byte count, threshold
- LatencyMonitor: defaults, lifecycle, live probe, status enum

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve three bugs found in code review

- fix(teardown): pass skipReapply:true when close() is called from teardown()
  to prevent the deferred RAF from overwriting the restored original style
- fix(overlay-rotate): guard displayAR calculation against NaN when video
  metadata is not yet loaded; use container dimensions as fallback
- fix(resize): clamp both width and height to 90% of viewport during manual
  resize so portrait videos can't push the overlay off-screen
- fix(videoDetector): add ResizeObserver to catch videos that are already in
  the DOM but have zero dimensions at script init time (fixes intermittent
  missing controls without page refresh)

perf(maglev): promote write-only globals in loop optimizer

Previously, promote_globals_in_loop() only promoted globals with BOTH
LoadGlobal and StoreGlobal in the loop body. Write-only globals (e.g.
'last' in object_creation) were not promoted, paying full
jit_runtime_sta_global stub cost per iteration.

Now any global stored inside the loop gets promoted to a Phi node.
Write-only globals use the StoreGlobal's feedback_slot to create the
preheader LoadGlobal, with an empty load_ids vec since there are no
original LoadGlobal nodes to replace.

commit.message:
Reduce code-review skill token usage (~29% smaller) (#126779)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was generated with the assistance of Copilot.

## Reduce code-review skill token usage (~29% smaller)

We've been seeing several 20-minute timeouts during code review runs. To
my knowledge this is a hard limit for GitHub agentic workflows. The
`code-review` SKILL.md is the largest skill file in dotnet/runtime at
~68KB (~17K tokens), and reducing its size should help the model work
more efficiently within the available time. This PR removes ~20KB (~5K
tokens) of low-value content without losing any review rules or
procedures.

### Changes

**1. Remove developer quote examples (−19KB)**

The skill contained ~150 indented blockquote lines (`> "..."`) with real
maintainer review quotes used as phrasing examples under each rule
bullet. These illustrated *how* to phrase feedback but did not define
*what* to check. All actual review rules, severity guidance, and
actionable instructions are preserved.

**2. Extract API Approval Verification into a separate file (−3KB from
always-loaded context)**

The API Approval Verification procedure is a self-contained
sub-procedure only needed when a PR introduces new public API surface.
It is now in `api-approval-check.md` alongside SKILL.md, loaded on
demand.

- Step 0 and Step 2 both contain explicit **MUST-load** instructions
with the exact file path
- A fail-closed clause ensures that if the file cannot be loaded, the
agent reports an error and blocks the review rather than silently
skipping verification
- The extracted file is fully self-contained with all three verification
steps

**3. Tweaked one line to note that always initialize is a managed only
rule**

### Impact

| Metric | Before | After | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| SKILL.md size | 68.9 KB | 48.7 KB | −29.3% |
| Always-loaded tokens | ~17.2K | ~12.2K | −5K |
| Review rules/procedures | All | All | None removed |
| API verification | Inline | On-demand file | Loaded when needed |

### Context: skill sizes in dotnet/skills

For reference, the 78 SKILL.md files in `dotnet/skills` average ~10.8KB
(~2,700 tokens), with the largest at ~36KB (~9,000 tokens). The runtime
code-review skill at 48.7KB post-trim is still larger than any of those,
but it covers a comprehensive multi-step review procedure rather than a
single-purpose task.

commit.message:
Fix improper indent on West Workspace and Project Config View (#433)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace path separators in tree item IDs to prevent incorrect indentation

VS Code's internal tree handle construction uses `/` as a hierarchy
separator. When TreeItem IDs contain `/` or `\` (from file paths or
as deliberate separators), VS Code infers phantom parent-child

commit.message:
perf(maglev): add Int32/CheckedSmi variants to is_arithmetic_compatible_consumer

After the truncation pass converts Generic* to Int32* nodes, the
smi_guarded analysis was unable to propagate through Phi nodes whose
consumers had been truncated. This blocked speculative Smi optimization
for loop counters feeding into Int32 arithmetic.

Add 21 Int32/CheckedSmi variants to is_arithmetic_compatible_consumer
so that smi_guarded propagation works correctly after truncation.
